LAW AND MOTION 301, PLAINTIFF DANIEL POWELL'S MOTION TO STRIKE DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. PLAINTIFF DANIEL POWELL'S MOTION TO STRIKE DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS DENIED BECAUSE THE MOTION WAS FILED LESS THAN 16 DAYS BEFORE THE HEARING IN VIOLATION OF CCP 1005. ADDITIONALLY, THE MOTION IS DENIED AS MOOT, GIVEN THE COURT HAS ALREADY RULED ON THE DEMURRER THAT PLAINTIFF SEEKS TO STRIKE FROM THE RECORD. PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER THAT IS VERBATIM WITH THE COURT'S RULING. JUDGE: JOSEPH M. QUINN, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, NOT REPORTED. (301/JMQ)

MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS
Off Calendar Aug-27-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS

The City and County of San Francisco's motion for judgment on the pleadings in favor of London Breed, its former mayor, is granted without leave to amend. Government Code, section 820.9, provides "Members of city councils, mayors, . . . and members of locally appointed or elected advisory bodies are not vicariously liable for injuries caused by the act or omission of the public entity or advisory body." The operative complaint does not plead any nonconclusory facts about Breed's participation in the events of the case, and plaintiff Daniel Powell offers no facts he could plead on amendment. If employees of the City committed torts against Powell, the City itself may be vicariously liable but section 820.9 precludes vicarious liability by the mayor.

The City further seeks to stay this case because it is based on the same incident and similar claims that give rise to an earlier lawsuit by Powell, Powell v. San Francisco Fire Department et al., San Francisco Superior Court Case No. CGC-24-616508. The court takes judicial notice of the court records of that case and concludes that the actions do indeed arise out of the same events. "The pendency of another earlier action growing out of the same transaction and between the same parties is a ground for abatement of the second action." (Leadford v. Leadford (1992) 6 Cal.App.4th 571, 574.) The court therefore issues an interlocutory judgment of abatement and stays this action pending the resolution of Case No. CGC-24-616508.

Proposed order submitted.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (301/CVA)

Off Calendar Dec-13-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF DANIEL POWELL'S MOTION TO STRIKE DEMURRER to 1ST Amended COMPLAINT.

Plaintiff Daniel Powell's Motion to Strike Demurrer to First Amended Complaint is DENIED because the motion was filed less than 16 days before the hearing in violation of CCP 1005. Additionally, the motion is denied as moot, given the court has already ruled on the demurrer that Plaintiff seeks to strike from the record.

Prevailing party to submit a proposed order that is verbatim with the Court's ruling.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (301/JMQ)

LAW AND MOTION
Off Calendar Nov-21-2024 Sustained
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. JOSE A. ZELIDON-ZEPEDA, Esq. of the San Francisco City Attorney's Office (Tel: 415-355-3312) for defendants City and County of San Francisco, and London Breed;
No other appearances made in this matter.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANTS CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, AND LONDON BREED'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT.

The Court notes it received a notice of contest from the plaintiff. There being no appearance by the plaintiff at today's hearing, the Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendants' demurrer to the first amended complaint (FAC) is sustained with leave to amend. The FAC does not state facts sufficient to constitute a cause of action. (Code of Civil Procedure 430.10(e); see also People v. Sup. Ct. (1992) 5 Cal.App.4th 1480, 1486 [Judicial Council form complaint no more immune from demurrer than any other that fails to meet pleading requirements].)

Order signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: S. Kane; Proceedings Record by "For the Record." (302/RBU)
